input_dict.agent_role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

task_context.question: Who is able to fill a larger variety of roles in the entertainment industry, Dave Moody or Ransom Riggs?
task_context.agent_role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

output.query: Retrieve the following Wikipedia pages and extract the requested fields: Pages to fetch: 1. "Dave Moody" — if this is a disambiguation page, follow the link to the Dave Moody who is a musician/film director/producer and fetch that article. 2. "The Moody Brothers" 3. "Dave Moody (sportscaster)" 4. "Ransom Riggs" 5. (Optional) "No Limit Kids" (film) 6. (Optional) "Praise Band: The Movie" For each fetched page, provide: - Page title and URL. - Infobox: verbatim "Occupation" field. - Lead: the first 1–2 sentences of the article verbatim. - Any sections titled Career / Filmography / Discography / Works / Selected credits / Filmography or similar — copy the lines that state specific roles/credits (e.g., "directed by Dave Moody", "produced by", "provided the musical score", "writer and filmmaker"). - If the page is a disambiguation page, indicate which linked entry corresponds to the entertainment Dave Moody and fetch that entry. - A deduplicated list of all distinct entertainment occupations identified on that page (single-line comma-separated). - If available, list years active and notable works that support the occupations (e.g., film titles, albums). Return results grouped by person/page so I can directly compare the distinct occupations for: - Dave Moody (entertainment/musician/filmmaker) - Ransom Riggs End query.

hop2_doc_snippets: Praise Band: The Movie | Praise Band: The Movie is a 2008 faith-based film by Elevating Entertainment Motion Pictures.  It starred George Hamilton IV, Adam Melton and James Dana Bryan.  The screenplay was written by Joshua Moody and the film was produced and directed by Dave Moody.

No Limit Kids: Much Ado About Middle School | No Limit Kids: Much Ado About Middle School is a 2010 direct-to-video family comedy film.  It starred Bill Cobbs, Lee Meriwether, Blake Michael, Celeste Kellogg and Ashton Harrell.  The screenplay was written by Joshua Moody and the film was directed by Dave Moody for Elevating Entertainment Motion Pictures, who also provided the musical score for the film.

Dave Moody (sportscaster) | David Wayne "Dave" Moody is an American motorsports commentator who works for the Motor Racing Network.  He is the lead turn announcer for MRN and has worked for the radio network on and off since 1983.  He moved to MRN full-time in 1997 and became the lead turn announcer in 2001.  Since 2003, Moody has been the host of the nationally syndicated Sirius XM show "Sirius XM Speedway" on Sirius XM NASCAR Radio.

Please follow the entry format.

Dave Page | Dave Page (born 1939) is considered the world's leading expert on mountaineering footwear history, as well as an expert cobbler (a hiking boot repairman).  He is a former history professor at the University of Washington and is a cobbler based in Seattle, Washington, United States.  He has been resoling mountain boots since 1968.  He works closely with boot manufacturers worldwide doing a wide range of innovative resoling and repair work.  Forensic anthropologists called on Dave Page ...[truncated]
left_state.summary_2: Dave Moody is credited as producer and director (Praise Band: The Movie — "produced and directed by Dave Moody") and is part of The Moody Brothers (an Americana country music trio); Ransom Riggs is described in the context as an American writer and filmmaker. Thus Dave Moody occupies a larger variety of entertainment roles (producer, director, musician) than Ransom Riggs (writer, filmmaker).

output.query: Dave Moody Who is able to fill a larger variety of roles in the entertainment industry, Dave Moody or Ransom Riggs?

hop2_titles: No Limit Kids: Much Ado About Middle School
hop2_doc_snippets: The Moody Brothers | The Moody Brothers are an Americana country music trio who gained prominence in 1985 when they were nominated for a Grammy Award for their instrumental performance of the classic fiddle tune "Cotton Eyed Joe".  This trio of brothers, Carlton Moody, Dave Moody, and Trent Moody were nominated for a second Grammy in 1990 and won three International Country Music Awards in Europe.

Dave Moody (sportscaster) | David Wayne "Dave" Moody is an American motorsports commentator who works for the Motor Racing Network.  He is the lead turn announcer for MRN and has worked for the radio network on and off since 1983.  He moved to MRN full-time in 1997 and became the lead turn announcer in 2001.  Since 2003, Moody has been the host of the nationally syndicated Sirius XM show "Sirius XM Speedway" on Sirius XM NASCAR Radio.

Praise Band: The Movie | Praise Band: The Movie is a 2008 faith-based film by Elevating Entertainment Motion Pictures.  It starred George Hamilton IV, Adam Melton and James Dana Bryan.  The screenplay was written by Joshua Moody and the film was produced and directed by Dave Moody.

No Limit Kids: Much Ado About Middle School | No Limit Kids: Much Ado About Middle School is a 2010 direct-to-video family comedy film.  It starred Bill Cobbs, Lee Meriwether, Blake Michael, Celeste Kellogg and Ashton Harrell.  The screenplay was written by Joshua Moody and the film was directed by Dave Moody for Elevating Entertainment Motion Pictures, who also provided the musical score for the film.

Dave Moody | David B. Moody is an artist, producer, songwriter and filmmaker from North Carolina.  His instrumental proficiency has earned him two Grammy Award Nominations and three International CMA Awards as a member of The Moody Brothers.  After becoming regular performers on the Grand Ole Opry and touring extensively throughout Europe in the 80s, the trio of Carolina brothers signed a contract with the Walt Disney Company in 1992 to open and perform their own nightly concerts at Disneyland P ...[truncated]

Moody's Manual | Moody's Manual is a series of manuals published by the Moody's Corporation.  It was first published in 1900 by John Moody, nine years before he founded Moody's.  Initially called "Moody's Manual of Industrial and Miscellaneous Securities", it was later superseded by "Moody's Manual of Railroads and Corporation Securities", then by "Moody's Analyses of Investments".

Moody Memorial Library | Moody Memorial Library is one of the two central libraries at Baylor University located in Waco, Texas.  Jesse H. Jones Library is connected to Moody by two hallways and serves as the second of the two central libraries at Baylor.  Moody is the larger of the two central libraries.
right_state.summary_2: Dave Moody fills a larger variety of entertainment roles than Ransom Riggs: the “Dave Moody” passage lists him as an artist, producer, songwriter and filmmaker (and “Praise Band: The Movie” and “No Limit Kids: Much Ado About Middle School” note he produced and directed and provided the musical score), while the “Ransom Riggs” passage describes him as an "American writer and filmmaker".

request.rendered_prompt:
You are working with a functional semantic distance over states of one
agent in a multi-agent pipeline.

Each state contains the selected agent's output and may also contain the
downstream consequences produced by that output while the rest of the
pipeline is held fixed. The agent output is the primary object of the
transition. Downstream evidence and metrics are supporting evidence about
its functional effect.

The distance is not surface text edit distance and is not a correctness
score. It measures how much task-relevant behavior and information must
change to move from one state to another.

Judge distance using:
1. Agent-output behavior: what the output is trying to accomplish for the
   selected agent's role.
2. Task-relevant information: which entities, relations, evidence,
   conclusions, or answer constraints are preserved, added, removed, or
   corrected.
3. Downstream consequence: how the output changes retrieval, evidence,
   answerability, or information available to later agents.
4. Transition utility: whether a state is a meaningful intermediate step
   between two endpoint states.

Do not infer distance from text length, token count, document count, or
lexical overlap. Do not treat a state as closer merely because its metric
or final answer is better. Metrics are evidence of functional consequence,
not the distance definition itself.

A midpoint output o_mid between o_left and o_right must:
- preserve useful task-relevant behavior or information from o_left;
- make concrete semantic progress toward o_right;
- differ meaningfully from both endpoint outputs;
- not merely paraphrase or copy o_left;
- not simply copy o_right;
- represent two smaller functional transitions than the original edge.

Compare all adjacent transitions jointly and select the single edge with
the largest functional semantic distance.

Use the same distance definition for every edge. If two edges are
functionally tied, select the lower edge_index.

Agent:
query

Agent role:
Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.
